title: Multiscale Switch for Semi-Supervised and Contrastive Learning in Medical Ultrasound Image Segmentation
comment: This is the author-submitted LaTeX version with original typesetting. The final published version (with IEEE production formatting and layout changes) is available at http://doi.org/10.1109/TNNLS.2026.3669814 under CC BY 4.0 license
summary: Medical ultrasound image segmentation faces significant challenges due to limited labeled data and characteristic imaging artifacts including speckle noise and low-contrast boundaries. While semi-supervised learning (SSL) approaches have emerged to address data scarcity, existing methods suffer from suboptimal unlabeled data utilization and lack robust feature representation mechanisms. In this paper, we propose Switch, a novel SSL framework with two key innovations: (1) Multiscale Switch (MSS) strategy that employs hierarchical patch mixing to achieve uniform spatial coverage; (2) Frequency Domain Switch (FDS) with contrastive learning that performs amplitude switching in Fourier space for robust feature representations. Our framework integrates these components within a teacher-student architecture to effectively leverage both labeled and unlabeled data. Comprehensive evaluation across six diverse ultrasound datasets (lymph nodes, breast lesions, thyroid nodules, and prostate) demonstrates consistent superiority over state-of-the-art methods. At 5\% labeling ratio, Switch achieves remarkable improvements: 80.04\% Dice on LN-INT, 85.52\% Dice on DDTI, and 83.48\% Dice on Prostate datasets, with our semi-supervised approach even exceeding fully supervised baselines. The method maintains parameter efficiency (1.8M parameters) while delivering superior performance, validating its effectiveness for resource-constrained medical imaging applications. The source code is publicly available at https://github.com/jinggqu/Switch

AI.tldr: 提出了一种名为Switch的新型半监督学习框架，用于医学超声图像分割，通过多尺度开关(MSS)和频域开关(FDS)策略有效利用未标注数据，在仅5%标注比例下在6个超声数据集上取得了优异的分割性能。
AI.motivation: 医学超声图像分割面临标注数据有限、 speckle噪声和低对比度边界等挑战，现有半监督学习方法对未标注数据的利用不充分，缺乏鲁棒的特征表示机制。
AI.method: 提出两种创新策略：1) 多尺度开关(MSS)采用分层patch混合实现均匀空间覆盖；2) 频域开关(FDS)结合对比学习在傅里叶域进行振幅切换获取鲁棒特征。框架整合于teacher-student架构中有效利用标注和未标注数据。
AI.result: 在6个不同超声数据集(淋巴结、乳腺病变、甲状腺结节和前列腺)上验证，5%标注比例下在LN-INT数据集达80.04% Dice，DDTI数据集达85.52% Dice，Prostate数据集达83.48% Dice，半监督方法甚至超越全监督基线，且保持参数量高效(1.8M)。
AI.conclusion: Switch框架在资源受限的医学影像应用中具有显著优势，能在极低标注成本下实现高性能分割，为医学超声图像分割提供了一种有效的半监督学习方案。

relevance_reason: This is a medical imaging paper about ultrasound image segmentation, completely unrelated to the research profile which focuses on Computer Graphics for 3D motion generation from 2D video. The application domains are entirely different - medical imaging vs. video-to-3D motion synthesis. The methods (semi-supervised learning for medical image segmentation) have no relevance to video generation models, 3D motion generation, or visual guidance features for motion synthesis. This paper addresses medical image segmentation challenges, not any of the pain points mentioned in the profile.

title: Serendipity by Design: Evaluating the Impact of Cross-domain Mappings on Human and LLM Creativity
summary: Are large language models (LLMs) creative in the same way humans are, and can the same interventions increase creativity in both? We evaluate a promising but largely untested intervention for creativity: forcing creators to draw an analogy from a random, remote source domain (''cross-domain mapping''). Human participants and LLMs generated novel features for ten daily products (e.g., backpack, TV) under two prompts: (i) cross-domain mapping, which required translating a property from a randomly assigned source (e.g., octopus, cactus, GPS), and (ii) user-need, which required proposing innovations targeting unmet user needs. We show that humans reliably benefit from randomly assigned cross-domain mappings, while LLMs, on average, generate more original ideas than humans and do not show a statistically significant effect of cross-domain mappings. However, in both systems, the impact of cross-domain mapping increases when the inspiration source becomes more semantically distant from the target. Our results highlight both the role of remote association in creative ideation and systematic differences in how humans and LLMs respond to the same intervention for creativity.

AI.tldr: 研究比较了大型语言模型（LLMs）与人类在创意生成上的表现，并测试了跨域映射（从随机远程源领域进行类比）是否能提升创造力。人类和LLMs为10种日常产品生成创新特性，分别在两种提示条件下：跨域映射（要求从随机分配的资源如章鱼、仙人掌、GPS等翻译特性）和用户需求（针对未满足的用户需求提出创新）。结果显示人类能从随机跨域映射中获益，而LLMs生成的创意想法比人类更原创，但跨域映射的效果在统计上不显著。然而，两种系统在跨域映射的灵感来源与目标产品语义距离越远时，效果都更好。研究强调了远程关联在创意构思中的作用，并揭示了人类和LLMs对同一创造力干预措施的系统性差异。
AI.motivation: 探索大型语言模型是否像人类一样具有创造力，以及相同的创造力干预措施是否对两者都有效。具体检验跨域映射（从随机、远程源领域进行类比）这一有前景但尚未充分验证的创造力干预方法。
AI.method: 人类参与者和LLMs为10种日常产品（如背包、电视）在两种提示条件下生成新颖特性：(1)跨域映射条件，要求从随机分配的资源（如章鱼、仙人掌、GPS）翻译特性；(2)用户需求条件，要求提出针对未满足用户需求的创新。比较两种条件下创意产出的原创性。
AI.result: 人类从随机分配的跨域映射中可靠获益；LLMs平均生成比人类更原创的想法，但跨域映射的效果在统计上不显著。然而，在两种系统中，当灵感来源与目标产品的语义距离越远时，跨域映射的影响都增加。
AI.conclusion: 研究强调了远程关联在创意构思中的作用，并揭示了人类和LLMs对同一创造力干预措施存在系统性差异。LLMs可能已经内置了跨域映射能力，因此额外的干预效果有限。

relevance_reason: This paper is about creativity research comparing LLMs vs humans in generating creative product features using cross-domain mappings. It has NO relevance to the research profile. The application domain is completely different - this is cognitive science/creativity research, not Computer Graphics. It does NOT address any of the pain points: not about 3D motion extraction from 2D video, not about video generation quality control, not about visual features driving 3D motion synthesis, not about bridging 2D-3D gap. Methods are also completely different - no video generation models, no 3D motion generation, no visual feature extraction. Even though VLMs are mentioned in the profile, this paper uses LLMs for text-based creativity tasks, not for any visual or graphics application.

title: Multimodal Model for Computational Pathology:Representation Learning and Image Compression
summary: Whole slide imaging (WSI) has transformed digital pathology by enabling computational analysis of gigapixel histopathology images. Recent foundation model advances have accelerated progress in computational pathology, facilitating joint reasoning across pathology images, clinical reports, and structured data. Despite this progress, challenges remain: the extreme resolution of WSIs creates computational hurdles for visual learning; limited expert annotations constrain supervised approaches; integrating multimodal information while preserving biological interpretability remains difficult; and the opacity of modeling ultra-long visual sequences hinders clinical transparency. This review comprehensively surveys recent advances in multimodal computational pathology. We systematically analyze four research directions: (1) self-supervised representation learning and structure-aware token compression for WSIs; (2) multimodal data generation and augmentation; (3) parameter-efficient adaptation and reasoning-enhanced few-shot learning; and (4) multi-agent collaborative reasoning for trustworthy diagnosis. We specifically examine how token compression enables cross-scale modeling and how multi-agent mechanisms simulate a pathologist's "Chain of Thought" across magnifications to achieve uncertainty-aware evidence fusion. Finally, we discuss open challenges and argue that future progress depends on unified multimodal frameworks integrating high-resolution visual data with clinical and biomedical knowledge to support interpretable and safe AI-assisted diagnosis.

AI.tldr: 该综述全面调研了多模态计算病理学领域的最新进展，涵盖四个研究方向：自监督表示学习、 多模态数据生成与增强、参数高效微调与推理增强的少样本学习、以及多智能体协作推理。 重点分析了token压缩如何实现跨尺度建模，以及多智能体机制如何模拟病理医师的"思维链" 实现不确定性感知的证据融合。
AI.motivation: 推动计算病理学发展的核心挑战包括：WSI的超高分辨率带来计算难题、专家标注有限、多模态信息整合困难、以及模型透明度不足影响临床可信度。
AI.method: 系统综述方法，分析四个研究前沿：1)自监督表示学习与结构感知token压缩；2)多模态数据生成与增强；3)参数高效适配与推理增强少样本学习；4)多智能体协作推理实现可信诊断。
AI.result: 探讨了token压缩 Enables跨尺度建模，多智能体机制模拟病理医师Chain of Thought实现多放大倍数下不确定性感知证据融合。
AI.conclusion: 未来进展取决于统一的多模态框架，需整合高分辨率视觉数据与临床生物医学知识，以支持可解释、安全的AI辅助诊断。

relevance_reason: This paper is about computational pathology (medical imaging for disease diagnosis), which is entirely different from the research profile's domain of Computer Graphics/3D motion generation. Even though both involve multimodal learning and token compression, the application domains are completely separate - medical diagnosis vs. video-to-3D motion synthesis. This represents a fundamental domain mismatch with no practical relevance to the stated research goals.

title: LuMamba: Latent Unified Mamba for Electrode Topology-Invariant and Efficient EEG Modeling
summary: Electroencephalography (EEG) enables non-invasive monitoring of brain activity across clinical and neurotechnology applications, yet building foundation models for EEG remains challenging due to \emph{differing electrode topologies} and \emph{computational scalability}, as Transformer architectures incur quadratic sequence complexity. As a joint solution, we propose \textbf{LuMamba} (\textbf{L}atent \textbf{U}nified \textbf{Mamba}), a self-supervised framework combining topology-invariant encodings with linear-complexity state-space modeling, using LUNA's learned-query cross-attention mechanism for channel unification~\cite{luna}, and FEMBA's bidirectional Mamba blocks for efficient temporal modeling~\cite{femba}. Within this architecture, we provide the first systematic investigation of the Latent-Euclidean Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture (LeJEPA) for biosignal learning. Pre-trained on over 21,000 hours of unlabeled EEG from the TUEG corpus, LuMamba is evaluated on five downstream tasks spanning abnormality detection, artifact recognition, and mental condition classification across electrode configurations ranging from 16 to 26 channels. In the pre-training objective, masked reconstruction alone yields structured but less generalizable representations, while LeJEPA alone produces diffuse embeddings; combining both objectives achieves the most robust performance. With only 4.6M parameters, LuMamba attains 80.99\% balanced accuracy on TUAB and achieves state-of-art performance on Alzheimer's detection (0.97 AUPR), while requiring \textbf{377$\times$ fewer FLOPS} than state-of-art models at equivalent sequence lengths and scaling to \textbf{12$\times$ longer sequences} before reaching typical GPU memory limits. Code is available at https://github.com/pulp-bio/biofoundation

AI.tldr: LuMamba是一个用于EEG的自监督基础模型，结合了拓扑不变编码与线性复杂度状态空间建模，在21,000小时TUEG数据上预训练，仅用4.6M参数就在多个下游任务取得最先进性能，且计算效率比Transformer模型高377倍。
AI.motivation: 构建EEG基础模型面临两大挑战：1) 不同电极拓扑结构导致的数据异构性；2) Transformer架构的二次复杂度带来的计算可扩展性问题。需要一种既能统一不同电极配置、又能高效处理长序列的方法。
AI.method: 提出LuMamba框架，核心创新包括：1) 使用LUNA的学习查询交叉注意力机制实现通道统一，生成拓扑不变编码；2) 采用FEMBA的双向Mamba块进行高效时序建模；3) 首次系统研究LeJEPA（潜在欧几里得联合嵌入预测架构）在生物信号中的应用；4) 预训练采用掩码重建与LeJEPA联合目标。
AI.result: 1) 预训练目标对比：单独掩码重建产生结构化但泛化性差的表示；单独LeJEPA产生弥散的嵌入；两者结合获得最鲁棒性能；2) 下游任务：TUAB平衡准确率80.99%；阿尔茨海默病检测AUPR达0.97（最先进水平）；3) 效率：仅4.6M参数，377×更少FLOPS，可处理12倍更长序列。
AI.conclusion: LeJEPA与掩码重建的联合预训练策略对EEG表示学习至关重要。LuMamba成功解决了EEG基础模型中的拓扑异构性和计算效率问题，为大规模EEG数据分析提供了高效可行的方案。

relevance_reason: This paper is about EEG (brain signal) modeling for medical applications - completely unrelated to the Computer Graphics research profile focused on 3D motion generation from 2D video. While both mention "Mamba" architecture and "LeJEPA" (joint embedding predictive architecture), the application domains are entirely different: EEG/biomedical signals vs. video/visual motion synthesis. The paper addresses electrode topology in EEG, not visual features for 3D motion. This is a biomedical signal processing paper with no relevance to computer graphics or motion generation research.

title: Towards High-Quality Image Segmentation: Improving Topology Accuracy by Penalizing Neighbor Pixels
summary: Standard deep learning models for image segmentation cannot guarantee topology accuracy, failing to preserve the correct number of connected components or structures. This, in turn, affects the quality of the segmentations and compromises the reliability of the subsequent quantification analyses. Previous works have proposed to enhance topology accuracy with specialized frameworks, architectures, and loss functions. However, these methods are often cumbersome to integrate into existing training pipelines, they are computationally very expensive, or they are restricted to structures with tubular morphology. We present SCNP, an efficient method that improves topology accuracy by penalizing the logits with their poorest-classified neighbor, forcing the model to improve the prediction at the pixels' neighbors before allowing it to improve the pixels themselves. We show the effectiveness of SCNP across 13 datasets, covering different structure morphologies and image modalities, and integrate it into three frameworks for semantic and instance segmentation. Additionally, we show that SCNP can be integrated into several loss functions, making them improve topology accuracy. Our code can be found at https://jmlipman.github.io/SCNP-SameClassNeighborPenalization.

AI.tldr: SCNP是一种通过惩罚最差分类邻居来提高图像分割拓扑精度的高效方法，可集成到多种分割框架和损失函数中，在13个数据集上验证有效。
AI.motivation: 标准深度学习图像分割模型无法保证拓扑精度，无法正确保留连通分量数量，影响分割质量和后续量化分析的可靠性。现有方法存在集成繁琐、计算成本高或仅适用于管状结构等局限性。
AI.method: 提出SCNP方法，通过惩罚 logits 中分类最差的邻居像素，强制模型先改善像素邻域的预测，再改善像素本身的预测。方法可集成到语义分割和实例分割框架中，也可与多种损失函数结合使用。
AI.result: 在13个涵盖不同结构形态和图像模态的数据集上验证了有效性，成功集成到三个语义和实例分割框架中，并可使多种损失函数提升拓扑精度。
AI.conclusion: SCNP是一种高效、通用的拓扑精度提升方法，能够在不显著增加计算负担的情况下有效改善分割拓扑准确性，具有良好的通用性和可扩展性。

relevance_reason: This paper focuses on 2D image segmentation (semantic and instance segmentation) to improve topology accuracy. The application domain is completely different from the research profile which focuses on 3D motion generation from video, video generation models, and bridging 2D visual representations to 3D motion space. Image segmentation deals with labeling pixels in 2D images, while the research profile is about extracting/synthesizing 3D motion from video - these are entirely different application domains in computer graphics.

title: How Uncertainty Estimation Scales with Sampling in Reasoning Models
summary:
Uncertainty estimation is critical for deploying reasoning language models, yet remains poorly understood under extended chain-of-thought reasoning. We study parallel sampling as a fully black-box approach using verbalized confidence and self-consistency. Across three reasoning models and 17 tasks spanning mathematics, STEM, and humanities, we characterize how these signals scale.
  Both self-consistency and verbalized confidence scale in reasoning models, but self-consistency exhibits lower initial discrimination and lags behind verbalized confidence under moderate sampling. Most uncertainty gains, however, arise from signal combination: with just two samples, a hybrid estimator improves AUROC by up to $+12$ on average and already outperforms either signal alone even when scaled to much larger budgets, after which returns diminish. These effects are domain-dependent: in mathematics, the native domain of RLVR-style post-training, reasoning models achieve higher uncertainty quality and exhibit both stronger complementarity and faster scaling than in STEM or humanities.

AI.tldr: 研究了在扩展思维链推理场景下，如何通过黑盒方法估计推理语言模型的不确定性。发现自洽性和口头置信度都能随采样规模扩展，但结合两者的混合估计器仅用两个样本就能提升12%的AUROC。
AI.motivation: 扩展思维链推理的不确定性估计目前理解不足，对部署推理语言模型至关重要。
AI.method: 使用三种推理模型和17个任务（涵盖数学、STEM和人文领域），采用黑盒方法研究并行采样、口头置信度和自洽性三种不确定性估计信号。
AI.result: 自洽性和口头置信度都能扩展，但自洽性初始区分度较低，在中等采样下不如口头置信度。仅用两个样本的混合估计器平均提升AUROC达12%，优于单独使用任一信号，即使后者扩展到更大采样预算。
AI.conclusion: 混合估计器效果显著但存在领域依赖性：数学领域（RLVR后训练的原生领域）不确定性质量更高，信号互补性更强，扩展速度更快，STEM和人文领域则表现较弱。

relevance_reason: Completely irrelevant. This paper is about uncertainty estimation for Large Language Models (LLMs) in mathematical/reasoning tasks - an NLP application. The research profile is about Computer Graphics for 3D motion synthesis from video. While both mention "language models" or "VLM", this paper uses LLMs for text-based reasoning (math, STEM, humanities), NOT for visual understanding or video/3D motion generation. The application domains are entirely different - NLP reasoning vs. computer graphics/video generation.

title: D5P4: Partition Determinantal Point Process for Diversity in Parallel Discrete Diffusion Decoding
summary: Discrete diffusion models are promising alternatives to autoregressive approaches for text generation, yet their decoding methods remain under-studied. Standard decoding methods for autoregressive models, such as beam search, do not directly apply to iterative denoising, and existing diffusion decoding techniques provide limited control over in-batch diversity. To bridge this gap, we introduce a generalized beam-search framework for discrete diffusion that generates candidates in parallel and supports modular beam-selection objectives. As a diversity-focused instantiation, we propose D5P4, which formulates the selection step as MAP inference over a Determinantal Point Process. Leveraging a scalable greedy solver, D5P4 maintains multi-GPU compatibility and enables an explicit trade-off between model probability and target diversity with near-zero compute overhead. Experiments on free-form generation and question answering demonstrate that D5P4 improves diversity over strong baselines while maintaining competitive generation quality.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种针对离散扩散模型的广义束搜索解码框架，并设计了D5P4方法利用行列式点过程(DPP)实现批量内多样性控制，在保持生成质量的同时提升生成结果的多样性。
AI.motivation: 自回归模型的束搜索等标准解码方法不能直接适用于迭代去噪的离散扩散模型，而现有扩散解码技术对批量内多样性的控制能力有限。
AI.method: 提出广义束搜索框架，支持并行生成候选并实现模块化束选择目标；具体提出D5P4方法，将选择步骤建模为DPP上的MAP推理，采用可扩展的贪婪求解器，保持多GPU兼容性。
AI.result: 在自由形式生成和问答任务上的实验表明，D5P4相比强基线方法显著提升多样性，同时保持有竞争力的生成质量。
AI.conclusion: 该框架能够实现模型概率与目标多样性的显式权衡，且计算开销极小，为离散扩散模型的解码提供了新思路。

relevance_reason: This paper is about discrete diffusion decoding for TEXT generation (free-form generation, QA tasks), not Computer Graphics or 3D motion generation. While it uses diffusion models, the application domain is completely different - it's text generation diversity control, not video generation or 3D motion synthesis. The pain points in the research profile (extracting 3D motion from 2D video, quality control for video generation, visual guidance for motion synthesis) are not addressed at all. This is a NLP/text generation paper that has no relevance to computer graphics or motion generation research.

title: 6Bit-Diffusion: Inference-Time Mixed-Precision Quantization for Video Diffusion Models
summary: Diffusion transformers have demonstrated remarkable capabilities in generating videos. However, their practical deployment is severely constrained by high memory usage and computational cost. Post-Training Quantization provides a practical way to reduce memory usage and boost computation speed. Existing quantization methods typically apply a static bit-width allocation, overlooking the quantization difficulty of activations across diffusion timesteps, leading to a suboptimal trade-off between efficiency and quality. In this paper, we propose a inference time NVFP4/INT8 Mixed-Precision Quantization framework. We find a strong linear correlation between a block's input-output difference and the quantization sensitivity of its internal linear layers. Based on this insight, we design a lightweight predictor that dynamically allocates NVFP4 to temporally stable layers to maximize memory compression, while selectively preserving INT8 for volatile layers to ensure robustness. This adaptive precision strategy enables aggressive quantization without compromising generation quality. Beside this, we observe that the residual between the input and output of a Transformer block exhibits high temporal consistency across timesteps. Leveraging this temporal redundancy, we introduce Temporal Delta Cache (TDC) to skip computations for these invariant blocks, further reducing the computational cost. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method achieves 1.92$\times$ end-to-end acceleration and 3.32$\times$ memory reduction, setting a new baseline for efficient inference in Video DiTs.

AI.tldr: 本文针对Diffusion Transformer视频生成模型的高内存和计算成本问题，提出了一种推理时的NVFP4/INT8混合精度量化框架。该方法通过轻量级预测器动态分配量化精度，并利用时间冗余缓存进一步加速。
AI.motivation: Diffusion transformers在视频生成方面表现优异，但其实际部署受到高内存消耗和计算成本的严重限制。现有的后训练量化方法通常采用静态位宽分配，忽略了扩散时间步之间激活的量化难度差异，导致效率与质量的权衡不理想。
AI.method:
1. 发现模块输入输出差异与内部线性层量化敏感度之间存在强线性相关性
2. 设计轻量级预测器，动态为时间稳定层分配NVFP4以最大化内存压缩，为易变层保留INT8以确保鲁棒性
3. 观察到Transformer模块的输入输出残差在时间步之间具有高时间一致性，利用这一时间冗余引入Temporal Delta Cache (TDC)跳过不变块的计算
AI.result: 实现1.92倍端到端加速和3.32倍内存降低
AI.conclusion: 该自适应精度策略实现了激进的量化而不影响生成质量，为视频DiT的高效推理树立了新的基线。

relevance_reason: This paper addresses video generation model optimization through quantization (memory/computation reduction), while the research profile focuses on 3D motion synthesis from 2D video using visual guidance features. Although both involve video diffusion models, they are in DIFFERENT application domains: the paper targets general video generation deployment efficiency, whereas the profile targets human motion generation and 3D understanding. The methods are also fundamentally different - quantization optimization vs. motion synthesis pipelines. This is a model infrastructure/optimization paper that doesn't address any of the stated pain points about extracting 3D motion from 2D video or visual guidance for motion synthesis.

title: cuGenOpt: A GPU-Accelerated General-Purpose Metaheuristic Framework for Combinatorial Optimization
comment: 28 pages, 9 figures. Code available at https://github.com/L-yang-yang/cugenopt
summary:
Combinatorial optimization problems arise in logistics, scheduling, and resource allocation, yet existing approaches face a fundamental trade-off among generality, performance, and usability. We present cuGenOpt, a GPU-accelerated general-purpose metaheuristic framework that addresses all three dimensions simultaneously.
  At the engine level, cuGenOpt adopts a "one block evolves one solution" CUDA architecture with a unified encoding abstraction (permutation, binary, integer), a two-level adaptive operator selection mechanism, and hardware-aware resource management. At the extensibility level, a user-defined operator registration interface allows domain experts to inject problem-specific CUDA search operators. At the usability level, a JIT compilation pipeline exposes the framework as a pure-Python API, and an LLM-based modeling assistant converts natural-language problem descriptions into executable solver code.
  Experiments across five thematic suites on three GPU architectures (T4, V100, A800) show that cuGenOpt outperforms general MIP solvers by orders of magnitude, achieves competitive quality against specialized solvers on instances up to n=150, and attains 4.73% gap on TSP-442 within 30s. Twelve problem types spanning five encoding variants are solved to optimality. Framework-level optimizations cumulatively reduce pcb442 gap from 36% to 4.73% and boost VRPTW throughput by 75-81%.
  Code: https://github.com/L-yang-yang/cugenopt

AI.tldr: cuGenOpt是一个GPU加速的通用元启发式框架，解决组合优化问题中通用性、性能和可用性之间的权衡。该框架采用CUDA架构、统一编码抽象、双层自适应算子选择，并通过JIT编译和LLM建模助手提供Python API。
AI.motivation: 组合优化问题在物流、调度和资源分配等领域广泛应用，但现有方法在通用性、性能和可用性之间存在根本性的权衡问题。
AI.method: 1. 引擎层面：采用"一个block演化一个solution"的CUDA架构，包含统一编码抽象（排列、二进制、整数）、双层自适应算子选择机制和硬件感知资源管理；2. 扩展性层面：提供用户自定义算子注册接口；3. 可用性层面：JIT编译管道提供纯Python API，LLM建模助手将自然语言问题描述转换为可执行求解器代码。
AI.result: 在三个GPU架构(T4, V100, A800)上对五个主题套件的实验表明：cuGenOpt在通用性上优于MIP求解器数个数量级；在n≤150的实例上与专业求解器质量相当；在TSP-442上30秒内达到4.73%的gap；12种问题类型跨5种编码变体达到最优解；框架级优化使pcb442的gap从36%降至4.73%，VRPTW吞吐量提升75-81%。
AI.conclusion: cuGenOpt成功地在通用性、性能和可用性三个维度上同时取得突破，为组合优化问题提供了一个高效、易用的GPU加速求解框架。

relevance_reason: Completely irrelevant. This paper is about combinatorial optimization (TSP, VRPTW, logistics scheduling) using GPU-accelerated metaheuristic algorithms - a completely different field from Computer Graphics. It has nothing to do with video generation, 3D motion synthesis, Vision-Language Models, or visual feature extraction for motion generation. The application domain (operations research/logistics) is entirely unrelated to the research profile's focus on bridging 2D video and 3D motion generation in computer graphics.

title: WeNLEX: Weakly Supervised Natural Language Explanations for Multilabel Chest X-ray Classification
summary: Natural language explanations provide an inherently human-understandable way to explain black-box models, closely reflecting how radiologists convey their diagnoses in textual reports. Most works explicitly supervise the explanation generation process using datasets annotated with explanations. Thus, though plausible, the generated explanations are not faithful to the model's reasoning. In this work, we propose WeNLEX, a weakly supervised model for the generation of natural language explanations for multilabel chest X-ray classification. Faithfulness is ensured by matching images generated from their corresponding natural language explanations with original images, in the black-box model's feature space. Plausibility is maintained via distribution alignment with a small database of clinician-annotated explanations. We empirically demonstrate, through extensive validation on multiple metrics to assess faithfulness, simulatability, diversity, and plausibility, that WeNLEX is able to produce faithful and plausible explanations, using as little as 5 ground-truth explanations per diagnosis. Furthermore, WeNLEX can operate in both post-hoc and in-model settings. In the latter, i.e., when the multilabel classifier is trained together with the rest of the network, WeNLEX improves the classification AUC of the standalone classifier by 2.21%, thus showing that adding interpretability to the training process can actually increase the downstream task performance. Additionally, simply by changing the database, WeNLEX explanations are adaptable to any target audience, and we showcase this flexibility by training a layman version of WeNLEX, where explanations are simplified for non-medical users.

AI.tldr: WeNLEX是一种弱监督模型，用于为多标签胸部X光分类生成自然语言解释。通过在黑盒模型特征空间中匹配从解释生成的图像与原始图像来确保忠诚度，并通过与少量临床医生注释的解释进行分布对齐来保持合理性。该模型仅需每个诊断5个真实解释即可产生忠诚且合理的解释。
AI.motivation: 大多数工作使用带有解释注释的数据集显式监督解释生成过程，因此生成的解释虽然看似合理但不能忠实反映模型的推理过程。如何在弱监督条件下生成既忠诚（忠实于模型推理）又合理（符合人类认知）的自然语言解释是一个挑战。
AI.method: 1) 通过在黑盒模型特征空间中匹配从自然语言解释生成的图像与原始图像来确保忠诚度；2) 通过与临床医生注释的小型解释数据库进行分布对齐来保持合理性；3) 弱监督设置：仅需每个诊断5个真实解释；4) 可在事后解释和模型内解释两种设置下运行。
AI.result: 1) 在多个评估指标（忠诚度、可模拟性、多样性、合理性）上验证了模型效果；2) 仅需5个真实解释即可产生忠诚且合理的解释；3) 在模型内设置下，WeNLEX将独立分类器的AUC提高2.21%；4) 可通过更换数据库轻松适应不同目标受众（如为非医学用户生成简化解释）。
AI.conclusion: WeNLEX成功实现了在弱监督条件下生成忠诚且合理的自然语言解释，且添加可解释性到训练过程实际上可以提高下游任务性能。该方法具有灵活性，可适应不同受众群体，展示了可解释性与模型性能可以共赢的潜力。

relevance_reason: Completely irrelevant application domain. This paper is about medical imaging (chest X-ray classification with natural language explanations) - a healthcare/medical AI domain. The research profile is about Computer Graphics focused on 3D motion generation from 2D video (e.g., video generation models for human motion synthesis). Even though both use Vision-Language Models and feature extraction, the application domains are entirely different - medical image classification vs. computer graphics/3D motion synthesis. This would be like comparing apples to oranges - no relevance to the stated research profile.

title: Box Maze: A Process-Control Architecture for Reliable LLM Reasoning
comment: 10 pages, 5 tables, 0 figures. Conceptual architecture with preliminary simulation-based validation
summary:
Large language models (LLMs) demonstrate strong generative capabilities but remain vulnerable to hallucination and unreliable reasoning under adversarial prompting. Existing safety approaches -- such as reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) and output filtering -- primarily operate at the behavioral level and may lack explicit architectural mechanisms for enforcing reasoning process integrity.
  This paper proposes the Box Maze framework, a conceptual process-control architecture that decomposes LLM reasoning into three explicit layers: memory grounding, structured inference, and boundary enforcement. We introduce preliminary simulation-based evaluation involving progressive boundary erosion scenarios across multiple heterogeneous LLM systems (DeepSeek-V3, Doubao, Qwen). Results from n=50 adversarial scenarios suggest that explicit cognitive control layers may improve consistency in boundary maintenance, with architectural constraints reducing boundary failure rates from approximately 40% (baseline RLHF) to below 1% under adversarial conditions.
  While current validation is simulation-based, these preliminary results indicate that process-level control may offer a promising direction for improving reliability in large language model reasoning.

AI.tldr: 本文提出Box Maze框架，通过将LLM推理分解为记忆锚定、结构化推理和边界执行三个显式层来增强推理过程完整性。初步仿真结果表明，该架构可将对抗条件下的边界失败率从约40%降至1%以下。
AI.motivation: 大型语言模型在对抗性提示下容易产生幻觉和不可靠推理，现有安全方法如人类反馈强化学习(RLHF)和输出过滤主要在行为层面操作，缺乏显式的架构机制来强制执行推理过程完整性。
AI.method: 提出Box Maze框架，将LLM推理分解为三个显式层：记忆锚定(Memory Grounding)、结构化推理(Structured Inference)和边界执行(Boundary Enforcement)。在多个异构LLM系统(DeepSeek-V3、Doubao、Qwen)上进行了渐进式边界侵蚀场景的仿真评估，涵盖50个对抗场景。
AI.result: 初步结果表明，显式认知控制层可改善边界维护的一致性，架构约束将对抗条件下的边界失败率从约40%(基线RLHF)降至1%以下。
AI.conclusion: 虽然当前验证基于仿真，但这些初步结果表明过程级控制可能是提高大型语言模型推理可靠性的一个有前景的方向。

relevance_reason: Completely irrelevant. This paper is about LLM reasoning reliability and AI safety in NLP, not computer graphics or video/3D motion generation. The application domain is entirely different - it addresses LLM hallucination and adversarial prompts, while the research profile focuses on video generation models, 3D motion synthesis, and visual feature extraction for computer graphics. The methods are also unrelated (LLM reasoning architectures vs video generation and motion synthesis).

title: ProCal: Probability Calibration for Neighborhood-Guided Source-Free Domain Adaptation
summary: Source-Free Domain Adaptation (SFDA) adapts pre-trained models to unlabeled target domains without requiring access to source data. Although state-of-the-art methods leveraging local neighborhood structures show promise for SFDA, they tend to over-rely on prediction similarity among neighbors. This over-reliance accelerates the forgetting of source knowledge and increases susceptibility to local noise overfitting. To address these issues, we introduce ProCal, a probability calibration method that dynamically calibrates neighborhood-based predictions through a dual-model collaborative prediction mechanism. ProCal integrates the source model's initial predictions with the current model's online outputs to effectively calibrate neighbor probabilities. This strategy not only mitigates the interference of local noise but also preserves the discriminative information from the source model, thereby achieving a balance between knowledge retention and domain adaptation. Furthermore, we design a joint optimization objective that combines a soft supervision loss with a diversity loss to guide the target model. Our theoretical analysis shows that ProCal converges to an equilibrium where source knowledge and target information are effectively fused, reducing both knowledge forgetting and overfitting. We validate the effectiveness of our approach through extensive experiments on 31 cross-domain tasks across four public datasets. Our code is available at: https://github.com/zhengyinghit/ProCal.

AI.tldr: ProCal是一种无源域自适应方法，通过双模型协同预测机制动态校准邻域预测概率，解决现有方法过度依赖邻居预测相似性导致的知识遗忘和局部噪声过拟合问题。
AI.motivation: 现有无源域自适应方法过度依赖邻居间的预测相似性，这加速了源知识的遗忘，并增加了对局部噪声过拟合的敏感性。
AI.method: 提出ProCal概率校准方法，集成源模型的初始预测与当前模型的在线输出进行邻域概率校准，设计了结合软监督损失和多样性损失的联合优化目标。
AI.result: 在4个公共数据集的31个跨域任务上验证了方法有效性，实验表明ProCal能在保持源知识的同时有效适应目标域。
AI.conclusion: 理论分析表明ProCal收敛至源知识与目标信息有效融合的平衡状态，减少了知识遗忘和过拟合，代码已开源。

relevance_reason: This paper is about source-free domain adaptation and probability calibration for image classification tasks in computer vision. It has nothing to do with Computer Graphics, 3D motion generation, video generation models, or bridging 2D visual representations to 3D motion space. The application domain is completely different - it's focused on domain adaptation for recognition tasks, not motion synthesis or video generation.

title: SEAR: Simple and Efficient Adaptation of Visual Geometric Transformers for RGB+Thermal 3D Reconstruction
summary: Foundational feed-forward visual geometry models enable accurate and efficient camera pose estimation and scene reconstruction by learning strong scene priors from massive RGB datasets. However, their effectiveness drops when applied to mixed sensing modalities, such as RGB-thermal (RGB-T) images. We observe that while a visual geometry grounded transformer pretrained on RGB data generalizes well to thermal-only reconstruction, it struggles to align RGB and thermal modalities when processed jointly. To address this, we propose SEAR, a simple yet efficient fine-tuning strategy that adapts a pretrained geometry transformer to multimodal RGB-T inputs. Despite being trained on a relatively small RGB-T dataset, our approach significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods for 3D reconstruction and camera pose estimation, achieving significant improvements over all metrics (e.g., over 29\% in AUC@30) and delivering higher detail and consistency between modalities with negligible overhead in inference time compared to the original pretrained model. Notably, SEAR enables reliable multimodal pose estimation and reconstruction even under challenging conditions, such as low lighting and dense smoke. We validate our architecture through extensive ablation studies, demonstrating how the model aligns both modalities. Additionally, we introduce a new dataset featuring RGB and thermal sequences captured at different times, viewpoints, and illumination conditions, providing a robust benchmark for future work in multimodal 3D scene reconstruction. Code and models are publicly available at https://www.github.com/Schindler-EPFL-Lab/SEAR.

AI.tldr: SEAR是一种针对RGB-热成像(RGB-T)多模态输入的简单高效微调策略,能够将预训练的视觉几何变换器适应到多模态RGB-T数据中,在3D重建和相机位姿估计任务上显著超越现有方法。
AI.motivation: 当前基于RGB数据预训练的视觉几何模型在处理混合传感模态(如RGB-热成像)时效果下降。虽然模型能很好地泛化到纯热成像重建,但在联合处理RGB和热成像模态时难以对齐两种模态。
AI.method: 提出SEAR微调策略,利用相对较小的RGB-T数据集对预训练的几何变换器进行适配,使模型能够对齐RGB和热成像两种模态。
AI.result: 在3D重建和相机位姿估计方面显著超越最先进方法,AUC@30提升超过29%,在低光照和浓烟等挑战性条件下仍能实现可靠的多模态位姿估计和重建,且推理时间几乎没有额外开销。
AI.conclusion: SEAR能够有效对齐RGB和热成像模态,提出的新RGB-T数据集为未来多模态3D场景重建研究提供了稳健的基准。代码和模型已公开可用。

relevance_reason: The paper addresses RGB+Thermal 3D reconstruction using visual geometric transformers, which is a completely different application domain. The research profile focuses on 3D MOTION GENERATION from 2D video priors (specifically video generation models like Wanxiang/Seedance), while this paper focuses on 3D RECONSTRUCTION from multi-sensor (RGB+thermal) input. These are distinct areas: motion generation creates new 3D animations, while reconstruction recovers 3D structure from images. The methods also differ - this uses geometric transformers for sensor fusion, not video generation or 3D motion synthesis models.

title: Rethinking Uncertainty Quantification and Entanglement in Image Segmentation
summary: Uncertainty quantification (UQ) is crucial in safety-critical applications such as medical image segmentation. Total uncertainty is typically decomposed into data-related aleatoric uncertainty (AU) and model-related epistemic uncertainty (EU). Many methods exist for modeling AU (such as Probabilistic UNet, Diffusion) and EU (such as ensembles, MC Dropout), but it is unclear how they interact when combined. Additionally, recent work has revealed substantial entanglement between AU and EU, undermining the interpretability and practical usefulness of the decomposition. We present a comprehensive empirical study covering a broad range of AU-EU model combinations, propose a metric to quantify uncertainty entanglement, and evaluate both across downstream UQ tasks. For out-of-distribution detection, ensembles exhibit consistently lower entanglement and superior performance. For ambiguity modeling and calibration the best models are dataset-dependent, with softmax/SSN-based methods performing well and Probabilistic UNets being less entangled. A softmax ensemble fares remarkably well on all tasks. Finally, we analyze potential sources of uncertainty entanglement and outline directions for mitigating this effect.

AI.tldr: 本文是一项关于医学图像分割中不确定性量化的综合实证研究，探讨了 aleatoric 不确定性(AU) 和 epistemic 不确定性(EU) 模型的组合，提出了一种量化不确定性纠缠的指标，并在下游UQ任务中进行评估。
AI.motivation: 不确定性量化在医学图像分割等安全关键应用中至关重要。AU和EU的分解可解释性和实用性受到二者之间显著纠缠的挑战，且尚不清楚不同AU和EU方法组合时的交互方式。
AI.method: 对广泛的AU-EU模型组合进行综合实证研究，提出不确定性纠缠量化指标，并在分布外检测、歧义建模和校准等下游UQ任务中进行评估。
AI.result: 分布外检测中，集成方法显示更低的纠缠和更优性能；歧义建模和校准中最佳模型与数据集相关，softmax/SSN方法表现良好，Probabilistic UNets纠缠较低；softmax集成在所有任务中表现优异。
AI.conclusion: 分析了不确定性纠缠的潜在来源，并提出了缓解该效应的方向。

relevance_reason: The paper is about medical image segmentation with uncertainty quantification, which is entirely different from the research profile focused on Computer Graphics for 3D motion generation from 2D video. Although both involve image processing, the application domain (medical imaging vs video/3D motion synthesis), the specific pain points (uncertainty in medical diagnosis vs quality control in video generation), and the end goals are completely unrelated. This represents a fundamental domain mismatch.

title: Perceptio: Perception Enhanced Vision Language Models via Spatial Token Generation
summary: Large Vision Language Models (LVLMs) excel at semantic understanding but struggle with fine grained spatial grounding, as the model must implicitly infer complex geometry without ever producing a spatial interpretation. We present Perceptio, a perception enhanced LVLM with 2D and 3D spatial reasoning abilities, enabled via explicit semantic segmentation tokens and depth tokens generated directly within the autoregressive sequence. Concretely, we (i) distill a VQVAE depth codebook from a strong monocular teacher to tokenize dense depth into compact sequences, and (ii) integrate SAM2 based semantic segmentation tokens and VQ-VAE depth tokens inside the LLM so the model first emits spatial tokens and then answers. To stabilize depth token generation, we introduce novel composite depth-token objectives (marker, token, and count losses) and a soft-merging technique for differentiable reconstruction. We adopt a multi-task co-training strategy across diverse datasets, letting the model learn perception tokens to tackle multiple downstream tasks. Building on InternVL, Perceptio achieves state-of-the-art performance across benchmarks: improving referring expression segmentation by +0.8/+1.4/+1.1 cIoU on RefCOCO/+/g HardBLINK spatial understanding accuracy by 10.3%, and MMBench accuracy by 1.0%, demonstrating that explicit spatial chain-of-thought materially strengthens spatial grounding in LVLMs.

AI.tldr: Perceptio是一个增强版的大视觉语言模型，通过在自回归序列中显式生成语义分割token和深度token来解决LVLMs在细粒度空间定位方面的不足。该模型基于InternVL构建，在多个基准上实现了最先进的性能。
AI.motivation: 大型视觉语言模型(LVLMs)在语义理解方面表现出色，但在细粒度空间定位方面存在困难。模型需要隐式推断复杂几何结构，却无法产生空间解释。这激发了研究者开发具有2D和3D空间推理能力的感知增强型LVLM。
AI.method: 1. 从强大的单目教师模型中蒸馏VQVAE深度码本，将密集深度 token化为紧凑序列；2. 集成SAM2基于语义分割的token和VQ-VAE深度token到LLM中，使模型先发射空间token再回答；3. 引入复合深度token目标（marker、token和count损失）和软合并技术以稳定深度token生成；4. 采用多任务协同训练策略。
AI.result: 在RefCOCO/+/g上 referring expression segmentation 分别提升+0.8/+1.4/+1.1 cIoU；BLINK空间理解准确率提升10.3%；MMBench准确率提升1.0%。
AI.conclusion: 显式的空间思维链（spatial chain-of-thought）能够显著增强LVLMs的空间定位能力，为多模态理解中的空间推理提供了有效途径。

relevance_reason: This paper focuses on enhancing Vision-Language Models for 2D spatial understanding tasks (semantic segmentation, depth estimation, referring expression segmentation). While it uses related techniques (VQVAE depth tokens, VLM), the application domain is fundamentally different from the research profile's focus on Computer Graphics and 3D motion generation. The paper addresses spatial reasoning in images/videos, not motion synthesis or video generation for 3D avatar control. This is a case of using similar methods (VLMs, depth tokens) in a completely different application domain.

title: Towards Interpretable Foundation Models for Retinal Fundus Images
comment: 11 pages, 3 figures, 2 tables, submitted to MICCAI 2026
summary: Foundation models are used to extract transferable representations from large amounts of unlabeled data, typically via self-supervised learning (SSL). However, many of these models rely on architectures that offer limited interpretability, which is a critical issue in high-stakes domains such as medical imaging. We propose Dual-IFM, a foundation model that is interpretable-by-design in two ways: First, it provides local interpretability for individual images through class evidence maps that are faithful to the decision-making process. Second, it provides global interpretability for entire datasets through a 2D projection layer that allows for direct visualization of the model's representation space. We trained our model on over 800,000 color fundus photography from various sources to learn generalizable, interpretable representations for different downstream tasks. Our results show that our model reaches a performance range similar to that of state-of-the-art foundation models with up to $16\times$ the number of parameters, while providing interpretable predictions on out-of-distribution data. Our results suggest that large-scale SSL pretraining paired with inherent interpretability can lead to robust representations for retinal imaging.

AI.tldr: Dual-IFM是一种专为视网膜影像设计的可解释基础模型，通过类别证据图提供局部可解释性，通过2D投影层提供全局可解释性，在80万张彩色眼底照片上预训练，性能与参数量多16倍的模型相当。
AI.motivation: 当前基础模型依赖可解释性有限的架构，这在医疗影像等高风险领域是关键问题。研究者提出需要在保证性能的同时提供可解释的预测，以增强医生对AI辅助诊断的信任度。
AI.method: 1)提出Dual-IFM框架，具有两种可解释性机制：类别证据图(局部可解释性)和2D投影层(全局可解释性)；2)在超过80万张来自不同来源的彩色眼底照片上进行自监督学习预训练；3)对分布外数据进行可解释性预测验证。
AI.result: 性能与最先进的基础模型相当，但参数量仅为其1/16；能够对分布外数据提供可解释的预测；学习到了可泛化的视网膜影像表示。
AI.conclusion: 大规模自监督预训练结合内在可解释性可以产生用于视网膜影像的鲁棒表示，为医疗AI的可解释性发展提供了新方向。

relevance_reason: Completely irrelevant. This paper is about medical imaging (retinal fundus images) for AI-assisted diagnosis, not Computer Graphics or 3D motion generation. The application domains are entirely different - medical AI vs. computer graphics/motion synthesis. While it mentions "2D projection layers," this has nothing to do with extracting 3D motion from 2D video or video generation models for computer graphics.

title: HORNet: Task-Guided Frame Selection for Video Question Answering with Vision-Language Models
summary: Video question answering (VQA) with vision-language models (VLMs) depends critically on which frames are selected from the input video, yet most systems rely on uniform or heuristic sampling that cannot be optimized for downstream answering quality. We introduce \textbf{HORNet}, a lightweight frame selection policy trained with Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) to learn which frames a frozen VLM needs to answer questions correctly. With fewer than 1M trainable parameters, HORNet reduces input frames by up to 99\% and VLM processing time by up to 93\%, while improving answer quality on short-form benchmarks (+1.7\% F1 on MSVD-QA) and achieving strong performance on temporal reasoning tasks (+7.3 points over uniform sampling on NExT-QA). We formalize this as Select Any Frames (SAF), a task that decouples visual input curation from VLM reasoning, and show that GRPO-trained selection generalizes better out-of-distribution than supervised and PPO alternatives. HORNet's policy further transfers across VLM answerers without retraining, yielding an additional 8.5\% relative gain when paired with a stronger model. Evaluated across six benchmarks spanning 341,877 QA pairs and 114.2 hours of video, our results demonstrate that optimizing \emph{what} a VLM sees is a practical and complementary alternative to optimizing what it generates while improving efficiency. Code is available at https://github.com/ostadabbas/HORNet.

AI.tldr: 本文提出HORNet，一个基于GRPO训练的轻量级帧选择策略，用于从视频中选择最相关的帧供VLM回答问题。该方法将帧选择任务形式化为"选择任意帧"(SAF)，与VLM推理解耦。HORNet仅需不到100万可训练参数，即可将输入帧减少99%，处理时间减少93%，同时在多个基准测试上提升答案质量。
AI.motivation: 当前的视频问答系统依赖均匀采样或启发式方法选择帧，这些方法无法针对下游问答质量进行优化。视频帧选择对VQA至关重要，但缺乏系统性的优化方法。
AI.method: 提出HORNet框架，使用Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO)训练轻量级帧选择策略（少于100万参数）。将任务形式化为"选择任意帧"(SAF)，将视觉输入筛选与VLM推理解耦。策略可跨VLM迁移使用。
AI.result: 在短形式基准测试中提升F1分数1.7%（MSVD-QA），在时间推理任务中比均匀采样提升7.3分（NExT-QA）。减少输入帧达99%，VLM处理时间减少93%。跨VLM迁移时可额外获得8.5%相对增益。评估涵盖6个基准测试、341,877个QA对和114.2小时视频。
AI.conclusion: 优化VLM"看到什么"是提升模型性能和效率的实用且互补的方法，与优化"生成什么"同等重要。GRPO训练的选择策略比监督学习和PPO具有更好的分布外泛化能力。

relevance_reason: This paper is about Video Question Answering (VQA) using VLMs for frame selection - a completely different application domain from Computer Graphics and 3D motion generation. While both use Vision-Language Models, the paper addresses NLP/QA tasks (selecting frames to improve question answering accuracy), not extracting 3D motion from 2D video, quality control of video generation, or visual guidance for 3D motion synthesis. This is essentially a different field (multimodal ML/NLP) that shares only the general use of VLMs as a method.

title: Motion-o: Trajectory-Grounded Video Reasoning
summary: Recent research has made substantial progress on video reasoning, with many models leveraging spatio-temporal evidence chains to strengthen their inference capabilities. At the same time, a growing set of datasets and benchmarks now provides structured annotations designed to support and evaluate such reasoning. However, little attention has been paid to reasoning about \emph{how} objects move between observations: no prior work has articulated the motion patterns by connecting successive observations, leaving trajectory understanding implicit and difficult to verify. We formalize this missing capability as Spatial-Temporal-Trajectory (STT) reasoning and introduce \textbf{Motion-o}, a motion-centric video understanding extension to visual language models that makes trajectories explicit and verifiable. To enable motion reasoning, we also introduce a trajectory-grounding dataset artifact that expands sparse keyframe supervision via augmentation to yield denser bounding box tracks and a stronger trajectory-level training signal. Finally, we introduce Motion Chain of Thought (MCoT), a structured reasoning pathway that makes object trajectories through discrete \texttt{<motion/>} tag summarizing per-object direction, speed, and scale (of velocity) change to explicitly connect grounded observations into trajectories. To train Motion-o, we design a reward function that compels the model to reason directly over visual evidence, all while requiring no architectural modifications. Empirical results demonstrate that Motion-o improves spatial-temporal grounding and trajectory prediction while remaining fully compatible with existing frameworks, establishing motion reasoning as a critical extension for evidence-based video understanding. Code is available at https://github.com/ostadabbas/Motion-o.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了空间-时间-轨迹(STT)推理任务，并设计了Motion-o模型和运动思维链(MCoT)推理框架，使视觉语言模型能够显式地理解和验证物体在连续观测间的运动轨迹。
AI.motivation: 现有视频推理研究虽然关注时空证据链，但忽略了物体如何在观测之间移动的推理。轨迹理解仍然是隐式的，难以验证。本文希望填补这一空白。
AI.method: 1)提出STT推理任务 formalization; 2)设计Motion-o模型，通过<motion/>标签显式表示每帧中物体的方向、速度和尺度变化; 3)构建轨迹接地数据集扩展，通过增强稀疏关键帧监督生成更密集的边界框轨迹; 4)提出MCoT结构化推理路径; 5)设计奖励函数使模型直接基于视觉证据进行推理，无需修改架构。
AI.result: Motion-o提升了时空接地和轨迹预测能力，与现有框架完全兼容，确立了运动推理作为证据驱动视频理解的关键扩展。
AI.conclusion: 运动推理是视频理解的重要扩展方向，Motion-o通过显式轨迹建模和结构化推理证明了其有效性和兼容性。

Using VLM for video reasoning is not the same as using VLM for 3D motion generation.

title: PromptHub: Enhancing Multi-Prompt Visual In-Context Learning with Locality-Aware Fusion, Concentration and Alignment
comment: Accepted to ICLR 2026. 17 pages, 11 figures, and 9 tables
summary: Visual In-Context Learning (VICL) aims to complete vision tasks by imitating pixel demonstrations. Recent work pioneered prompt fusion that combines the advantages of various demonstrations, which shows a promising way to extend VICL. Unfortunately, the patch-wise fusion framework and model-agnostic supervision hinder the exploitation of informative cues, thereby limiting performance gains. To overcome this deficiency, we introduce PromptHub, a framework that holistically strengthens multi-prompting through locality-aware fusion, concentration and alignment. PromptHub exploits spatial priors to capture richer contextual information, employs complementary concentration, alignment, and prediction objectives to mutually guide training, and incorporates data augmentation to further reinforce supervision. Extensive experiments on three fundamental vision tasks demonstrate the superiority of PromptHub. Moreover, we validate its universality, transferability, and robustness across out-of-distribution settings, and various retrieval scenarios. This work establishes a reliable locality-aware paradigm for prompt fusion, moving beyond prior patch-wise approaches. Code is available at https://github.com/luotc-why/ICLR26-PromptHub.

title: Translating MRI to PET through Conditional Diffusion Models with Enhanced Pathology Awareness
summary: Positron emission tomography (PET) is a widely recognized technique for diagnosing neurodegenerative diseases, offering critical functional insights. However, its high costs and radiation exposure hinder its widespread use. In contrast, magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) does not involve such limitations. While MRI also detects neurodegenerative changes, it is less sensitive for diagnosis compared to PET. To overcome such limitations, one approach is to generate synthetic PET from MRI. Recent advances in generative models have paved the way for cross-modality medical image translation; however, existing methods largely emphasize structural preservation while neglecting the critical need for pathology awareness. To address this gap, we propose PASTA, a novel image translation framework built on conditional diffusion models with enhanced pathology awareness. PASTA surpasses state-of-the-art methods by preserving both structural and pathological details through its highly interactive dual-arm architecture and multi-modal condition integration. Additionally, we introduce a novel cycle exchange consistency and volumetric generation strategy that significantly enhances PASTA's ability to produce high-quality 3D PET images. Our qualitative and quantitative results demonstrate the high quality and pathology awareness of the synthesized PET scans. For Alzheimer's diagnosis, the performance of these synthesized scans improves over MRI by 4%, almost reaching the performance of actual PET. Our code is available at https://github.com/ai-med/PASTA.

AI.tldr: PASTA是一个基于条件扩散模型的新型医学图像转换框架，用于从MRI合成PET图像，强调病理感知能力，解决了现有方法只注重结构保留而忽视病理细节的问题。
AI.motivation: PET虽然是诊断神经退行性疾病的重要技术，但成本高且存在辐射暴露；MRI虽无此限制但敏感性较低。现有从MRI生成合成PET的方法主要强调结构保留，忽略了病理感知的重要性。
AI.method: 提出PASTA框架，包含：1)高度交互的双臂架构；2)多模态条件集成；3)新型循环交换一致性；4)体生成策略。基于条件扩散模型构建，专门增强病理感知能力。
AI.result: PASTA在定性和定量结果上均优于现有最先进方法，能够生成高质量3D PET图像并保留病理细节。在阿尔茨海默病诊断中，合成PET比MRI性能提升4%，几乎达到实际PET水平。
AI.conclusion: PASTA成功实现了从MRI到PET的高质量跨模态医学图像转换，显著保留了病理信息，为神经退行性疾病诊断提供了有价值的工具。
